title: Friday Sermon 15 February 2002
description:
I have sustained an eye injury due to a fall during his early morning walk.  To avoid the unnecessary distress that I have to endure when others are distressed for me it s imiperative that those present and those watching him on TV do not make inquiries about the injury, do not write to me about it and only pray that it gets better soon, Inshallah.  It is just an injury and it will get better, Inshallah.

To continue with the theme of peace in the Friday sermon today with reference to Quranic verses and the Ahadith Allah the Exalted watches over the state of hearts of all and on the Day of Judgment, while Fire will consume some, others will be admitted in a state of peace and Allah manifests Himself to each person according to their capacity. 

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) said that he was most dismayed with that person who sought security and peace and having being granted it, destroyed it:*

By the Fig and the Olive.
By Mount Sinai.
By this Town the abode of peace.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Tīn [The Fig]: 2 – 4).

In many scholarly commentaries the reference to the Fig and the Olive herein is to the place of birth of Jesus (on whom be peace) and indeed Mount Sinai was where Moses (on whom be peace) was called upon and the 'abode of peace' was a reference to the Kaaba and Muhammad (peace and blessings be on him) and it was an evolutionary sign of the earlier prophets.

(Abu Abdullah al-Qurtubi.  'Tafsīr l-Qurtubi'.  Abul Qasim Mahmud ibn Umar al-Zamakhshari.  'Al Kashshaaf an Haqaiq al-Tanzil'. Fakhruddin al-Razi.  'Tafsir e Kabir: Mafatih al-Ghayb').

Surah Quraish makes particular mention of Allah granting security from fear and hunger, on several occasions my prayer made with the said Surah, brought me security and peace and indeed food, in the most astonishing manner.  Allah the Exalted displays evidence of His existence through such matters; otherwise He certainly cannot be seen per se.  The Holy Quran informed us that a time will come when the region where the Kaaba stands will prosper abundantly, indeed Prophet Abraham (peace be on him) had also prayed thus and today that land is most prosperous and affluent.  Those who make arrangements for the hereafter are granted prosperity in this world in that when a land is made exclusive to the prayer of God then despite being barren it is filled with Allah's grace and bounty.

Following are two of the several revelations of the Promised Messiah (on whom of peace):

Today you are a man of established position, and trustworthy, and My mercy is upon you in [matters of] the world and the Faith and you are among the people who have been granted the succour of Allah.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Ḥaqīqatul Waḥī' ['The Philosophy of Divine Revelation'].  Magazine Press, 1907.  'Rūhānī Khazāin: xxii'. English: Chaudhary Muhammad Ali.  Islam International Publications Ltd, 2018.  105).

God addressed me and said that I should inform my Community that those who believe and their belief is not adulterated with worldly considerations and is free from hypocrisy and cowardice and is not found wanting at any stage of obedience, such people are the favourites of God.  And God says that indeed these are the ones whose station is that of Truth.

(Ahmad.  'Risala al-Waiyyat' ['The Will'].  Magazine Press, 1905.  'R. K: xx'.  309.  English: 1997, 2005.  12).

* Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari.  'Sahih Bukhari'.  

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020215-UR.pdf
